topic: Minimax estimation rate for the ATE under structure-agnostic nuisance estimation when overlap decays one-sidedly near the propensity-score boundary. Focal object: the minimax rate threshold as a joint function of the overlap-decay exponent and nuisance smoothness, characterizing the regime where one-sided overlap decay strictly changes the achievable ATE rate relative to the bounded-overlap structure-agnostic result.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the sharp minimax ATE rate threshold under one-sided propensity-score overlap decay as a joint function of overlap-tail exponent and nuisance smoothness or black-box nuisance rates.

source_refs.claim: They compute ATE minimax lower bounds in pure and hybrid structure-agnostic classes, but the scanned formulation keeps inverse propensity weights in the bounded-overlap baseline rather than a one-sided tail-decay class.
source_refs.author: Bonvini, Kennedy, Dukes, and Balakrishnan

source_refs.claim: They frame ATE estimation with black-box nuisance estimators and prove structure-agnostic optimality for doubly robust learning, leaving weak-overlap tail decay outside the stated benchmark.
source_refs.author: Jin and Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: They show inverse-weight treatment-effect estimation can become irregular when weights grow without bound, with convergence rates depending on relative tail/support conditions.
source_refs.author: Khan and Tamer

source_refs.claim: Thresholded AIPW t-statistics handle weak overlap under high-level rate and tail conditions, but do not give a sharp minimax ATE phase diagram over smoothness and tail exponents.
source_refs.author: Dorn

source_refs.excerpt: does not handle learned local Riesz representers, adaptive bandwidth selection, uniform bands over x, or weak-overlap sequences
replace A-frontier-active with primitive empirical-process maximal-inequality + minimax lower-bound arguments
The untreated tail exponent affects the ATE profile but not this CATT/ATT contribution

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature has two adjacent pieces but not their sharp synthesis: structure-agnostic ATE minimax theory controls nuisance quality while treating overlap as regular, and irregular/weak-overlap papers analyze inverse-weight tails, thresholding, or asymptotic distributions without a matched lower-and-upper minimax rate over smoothness and one-sided tail decay. The prior CausalSmith catalogue confirms the same missing regime: the pointwise structure-agnostic proposal explicitly excluded weak-overlap sequences, the DML frontier entry banked the primitive empirical-process/lower-bound machinery as missing, and the overlap-tail proposal was partial-ID width rather than an estimation-rate theorem.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem proving matching upper and lower minimax risk bounds for the full-population ATE over a class with P(e(X) <= t) behaving like t^kappa on one side, Holder or black-box nuisance smoothness/rates, and an explicit elbow where the bounded-overlap structure-agnostic rate is preserved or strictly slowed.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest written template for motivating structure-agnostic ATE optimality and then isolating the missing overlap-tail regime.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: ATE needs flexible nuisance learning -> structure-agnostic optimality characterizes black-box nuisance rates under regular overlap -> one-sided overlap decay asks for the missing minimax phase diagram in kappa and smoothness.

method_exemplars.technique: Tail/support rate analysis for inverse-weight estimators, including how unbounded weights alter convergence rates.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The ATE score under one-sided overlap decay inherits the same inverse-weight irregularity, but needs a minimax rather than estimator-specific rate statement.

method_exemplars.technique: Localized fuzzy-hypothesis lower bounds that combine nonparametric-regression and functional-estimation perturbations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired ATE lower bound must similarly localize alternatives, but at the propensity-score boundary instead of a fixed CATE point.

method_exemplars.technique: Thresholded AIPW with weak-overlap tail controls and nuisance-rate bookkeeping for valid t-statistics.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The upper-bound side of a minimax theorem would likely adapt clipped or thresholded AIPW, with threshold choice tied to the tail exponent.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove a primitive weak-overlap DML frontier for ATE scores with tail-diverging Riesz weights rather than assuming active empirical-process and minimax lower-bound channels.

source_refs.claim: Automatic debiased machine learning estimates Riesz representers for causal and structural functionals, but its uniform-validity machinery is not a primitive weak-overlap minimax frontier for tail-diverging representers.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh

source_refs.claim: Regularized Riesz representer inference covers global and local parameters with finite-sample bounds, but does not identify the sharp primitive frontier under one-sided inverse-propensity tail divergence.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh

source_refs.claim: Weak-overlap doubly robust t-statistics impose tail and nuisance-rate restrictions sufficient for inference, leaving the primitive necessity frontier open.
source_refs.author: Dorn

source_refs.excerpt: derive the empirical-process upper scale and an actual matching lower-bound construction
replace those high-level frontier assumptions with primitive Holder/entropy/minimax arguments
Add a concrete non-corner report-packing witness for Conjecture 1 with a specified growing class H_n

open_problems.why_unsolved: Auto-DML and regularized Riesz papers give powerful orthogonal-score machinery, but the hard weak-overlap question is not merely learning a representer: the representer norm diverges in tail regions and the upper empirical-process scale and lower active alternatives must be derived from primitive entropy, smoothness, and tail assumptions. The prior DML frontier run was downgraded precisely because those channels were encoded in assumptions rather than proved, and one-sided overlap decay worsens that defect by making the Riesz/inverse-propensity channel tail-localized.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A primitive theorem deriving the empirical-process upper scale and matching Le Cam, Fano, or packing lower bounds for orthogonal ATE scores with one-sided tail-diverging weights from Holder, entropy, and overlap-tail assumptions, without assuming frontier-active alternatives.

writing_exemplar.why: Its narrative introduces Riesz representers as the general object behind debiased causal functionals, exactly the object that becomes unstable under weak overlap.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Many causal estimands are linear functionals -> debiasing requires Riesz representers -> weak overlap makes the ATE representer tail-divergent -> primitive upper and lower frontiers must replace high-level Riesz stability assumptions.

method_exemplars.technique: Regularized Riesz estimation and finite-sample orthogonal-score bounds with explicit regression and representer approximation errors.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired upper bound also decomposes regression and representer errors, but the representer norm must be controlled by tail exponent rather than bounded-overlap regularity.

method_exemplars.technique: Structure-agnostic lower bounds for black-box nuisance learners and doubly robust ATE scores.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The lower-bound channel should adapt the black-box nuisance-rate construction while adding tail-localized inverse-weight perturbations.

method_exemplars.technique: High-dimensional empirical-process Gaussian approximation and multiplier bootstrap controls.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The missing upper empirical-process scale is exactly a weighted maximal-inequality problem once the report class is tail-localized.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize whether one-sided propensity-tail decay changes the ATE minimax rate differently from two-sided decay and from ATT or CATT rates.

source_refs.claim: Their limited-overlap asymptotics send some propensities to zero or one and show slower convergence governed by effective local sample size, but not a smoothness-indexed ATE/ATT minimax separation.
source_refs.author: Hong, Leung, and Li

source_refs.claim: They derive alpha-stable limits for IPW and doubly robust estimators with many extreme propensity scores, showing inference can fail without strong support restrictions.
source_refs.author: Heiler and Kazak

source_refs.claim: Their irregular-identification rates depend on relative tail conditions, motivating but not resolving separate one-sided and two-sided causal estimand phase diagrams.
source_refs.author: Khan and Tamer

source_refs.excerpt: CATT/ATT use only the untreated treated-population tail, while ATE uses the sum of the two full-population tails
one-tail ATT phase follows by changing measure to P_{X|D=1}
critical overlap exponent controlling interval-width growth near e(x)=0 or 1

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing limited-overlap rate and limit-law papers often aggregate the problem as extreme propensities, finite strata with p_n approaching zero or one, or irregular inverse weights. They do not give separate minimax phase diagrams for decay near e=0 only, decay near e=1 only, and simultaneous two-tail decay, nor do they map those diagrams across ATE, ATT, and CATT. The failed overlap-tail proposal surfaced this one-tail/two-tail distinction but only for partial-ID width and was judged too definitional there; the estimation-rate analogue remains a real unresolved rate question.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A collection of minimax rate theorems for ATE, ATT, and CATT under separate tail laws P(e(X) <= t) and P(1 - e(X) <= t), proving exactly which tail exponent enters each estimand and giving examples where one-sided decay leaves one estimand at the bounded-overlap rate while slowing another.

writing_exemplar.why: It cleanly motivates limited overlap through propensities degenerating to zero or one and links degeneration to slower convergence.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Limited overlap creates small local treatment cells -> convergence rates depend on effective sample sizes in those cells -> causal estimands weight the two propensity tails differently -> one-sided and two-sided minimax rates must be separated.

method_exemplars.technique: Degenerating-propensity asymptotics and effective-sample-size rate calculations for average and local average treatment effects.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its p_n framework supplies the finite-sample-size intuition to refine into tail-exponent minimax rates.

method_exemplars.technique: Regular-variation and alpha-stable limit theory for untrimmed IPW and doubly robust scores.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Stable-law behavior diagnoses when one tail makes variance-like normal approximations fail, which helps locate minimax regime changes.

method_exemplars.technique: Relative support and inverse-weight tail comparisons in treatment-effect moment models.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired estimand-by-estimand theorem is a causal-functional sharpening of the same support-tail irregularity mechanism.

open_problems.open_problem: Extend the structure-agnostic nuisance-product lower-bound template to tail-localized ATE scores with learned outcome and propensity nuisances.

source_refs.claim: They derive Holder-smooth pointwise CATE minimax rates via localized lower bounds and a local-polynomial R-learner, but not a tail-localized global ATE lower bound under weak overlap.
source_refs.author: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman

source_refs.claim: Higher-order influence-function U-statistics yield optimal rates for some low-regularity functionals, but weak-overlap tail localization is not incorporated in the minimax boundary.
source_refs.author: Robins, Li, Mukherjee, Tchetgen Tchetgen, and van der Vaart

source_refs.claim: Hybrid smoothness can make first-order AIPW suboptimal for ATE, suggesting but not resolving how higher-order or localized lower-bound channels behave with weak-overlap tails.
source_refs.author: Bonvini, Kennedy, Dukes, and Balakrishnan

source_refs.excerpt: three primitive binary local Le Cam pairs give the Holder-bias, local-mean, and nuisance-product channels
Add an optimized-bandwidth corollary translating rho_n(h) into the best h_n rate
neither a global ATE Le Cam construction nor a smooth CATE oracle-rate proof directly yields the black-box nuisance product frontier

open_problems.why_unsolved: The pointwise CATE lower-bound template has three useful channels, but it is localized in covariate space under strong overlap. A weak-overlap ATE lower bound must instead place alternatives in propensity-score tail regions, calibrate chi-square distance through rare treatment cells, and make the ATE separation depend jointly on outcome perturbations, propensity perturbations, and the tail exponent. Existing HOIF and hybrid smoothness theory shows smooth structure can improve rates, but it does not settle whether those improvements survive the tail-localized inverse-weight variance and nuisance-product channels.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A tail-localized Le Cam, Assouad, or fuzzy-hypothesis lower-bound construction for ATE under one-sided overlap decay, with outcome and propensity nuisance perturbations whose chi-square distance and ATE separation yield the claimed joint tail/smoothness minimax threshold.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest motivation template for turning a causal effect target into a minimax phase diagram with an elbow and a lower-bound construction.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Heterogeneous causal effects need minimax optimality theory -> localized lower bounds reveal multiple rate channels and elbows -> weak-overlap ATE requires moving localization from x0 to the propensity boundary.

method_exemplars.technique: Fuzzy-hypothesis lower bounds combining regression and functional-estimation perturbations.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The same proof skeleton can be ported if the localized ball is reweighted by the propensity-tail law.

method_exemplars.technique: Higher-order influence-function U-statistics and bias-variance balancing for low-regularity functional estimation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: If first-order AIPW is not rate optimal in smooth weak-overlap classes, the upper-bound estimator will likely require a tail-weighted HOIF adaptation.

method_exemplars.technique: Hybrid structure-agnostic minimax lower and upper comparisons for ATE under nuisance smoothness.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its bounded-overlap hybrid class is the exact comparator whose lower-bound channels must be stress-tested under tail localization.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct an adaptive clipped or thresholded ATE estimator that is minimax-rate optimal when the one-sided overlap exponent and nuisance smoothness are unknown.

source_refs.claim: They show small denominators can make Gaussian approximations unreliable and propose inference robust to trimming choices, but not adaptive minimax optimality over tail and smoothness classes.
source_refs.author: Ma and Wang
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: They use trimming plus bias correction to retain double robustness and original causal targets under weak overlap, but their scan does not yield an adaptive minimax theorem.
source_refs.author: Ma, Sant'Anna, Sasaki, and Ura

source_refs.claim: Thresholded AIPW can support weak-overlap t-statistics under tail and nuisance restrictions, but the threshold is not shown to adapt minimax-rate optimally over unknown exponents and smoothness.
source_refs.author: Dorn

open_problems.why_unsolved: Weak-overlap estimation papers provide robust inference, trimming-bias correction, and sufficient threshold rules, but those rules are calibrated to distributional validity or specific asymptotic regimes rather than to a minimax risk oracle over unknown one-sided tail exponents and nuisance smoothness. Once the full ATE target is preserved, threshold selection must balance tail bias, nuisance-product bias, and inverse-weight variance uniformly over a continuum of classes.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An adaptive estimator, for example Lepski-selected clipped AIPW or aggregated thresholded higher-order DR, with oracle inequalities over kappa, nuisance smoothness, and dimension, plus a statement of whether adaptation is possible without logarithmic loss and whether confidence intervals can be uniformly valid over the same classes.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames weak-overlap trimming as a way to retain the original causal target via bias correction, which is the right motivation for adaptive threshold choice.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Weak overlap makes DR estimators unstable -> trimming improves variance but can change robustness or target behavior -> bias correction preserves the original causal estimand -> adaptive minimax thresholding asks how to choose the trim without knowing the tail exponent.

method_exemplars.technique: Robust inference across trimming thresholds under small-denominator asymptotics.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies threshold-indexed distributional controls that an adaptive risk proof would need to strengthen into oracle inequalities.

method_exemplars.technique: Bias-corrected trimmed doubly robust estimation preserving original treatment-effect targets.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The adaptive estimator should preserve the same target while selecting threshold as a function of unknown tail and nuisance rates.

method_exemplars.technique: Weak-overlap thresholded AIPW t-statistic analysis with explicit tail and nuisance-rate restrictions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its sufficient weak-overlap inference conditions give the natural grid over which a Lepski or aggregation selector would operate.

literature_map:
Hirano, Imbens, and Ridder (2003, Econometrica) and Hahn-style semiparametric ATE theory are the classical bounded-overlap efficiency benchmarks.
Crump, Hotz, Imbens, and Mitnik (2009, Biometrika) address limited overlap by choosing better-overlap subpopulations, changing the estimand rather than solving full-ATE minimax risk.
Khan and Tamer (2010, Econometrica) show inverse-weight identification can be irregular and slower than root-n, with rates governed by tail/support conditions.
Rothe (2017, Econometrica) explains limited-overlap inference through local sample sizes and robust confidence intervals.
Hong, Leung, and Li (2020, The Econometrics Journal) formalize finite-population limited overlap by sending propensities toward zero or one, producing slower effective-sample-size rates.
Ma and Wang (2020, JASA) analyze small denominators in IPW and robust inference over trimming choices.
Heiler and Kazak (2021, Journal of Econometrics) derive alpha-stable limits for IPW and doubly robust estimators with many extreme propensities.
Ma, Sant'Anna, Sasaki, and Ura (2023, revised 2026) use trimming-bias correction to retain double robustness and original causal targets under weak overlap.
Dorn (2025) is closest on weak-overlap doubly robust t-statistics, but it is an inference/thresholding result rather than a sharp minimax phase diagram.
Jin and Syrgkanis (2024) and Bonvini, Kennedy, Dukes, and Balakrishnan (2024) provide structure-agnostic ATE optimality and hybrid smoothness minimax baselines under regular overlap.
Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman (2024) provide a localized CATE minimax lower-bound template with elbow behavior, useful but not tail-localized for ATE.
Robins, Li, Mukherjee, Tchetgen Tchetgen, and van der Vaart (2017) show higher-order influence functions can deliver minimax functional rates; whether those gains survive weak-overlap tails is unresolved.
Mou, Ding, Wainwright, and Bartlett (2023) show no-overlap linear-functional risks depend on function-class geometry, suggesting a method but not a full observational ATE result with unknown propensity.
prior_proposal_map:
Direct qid stat_ate_overlap_decay/v1 has no same-qid proposal or review artifact yet.
Closest direct overlap predecessor is failed pid_overlap_tail_phase/v1: Conjectures 1-2 targeted overlap-tail phase laws, but reviews found one angle algebraically impossible and another a Manski/missing-mass regular-variation unfold rather than an estimation-rate theorem.
Banked pid_critical_overlap_exponent/v1 is a pipeline failure only; its README says not to treat it as mathematical rejection of the overlap-exponent topic.
The strongest structure-agnostic nuisance substrate is downgraded eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier/v1: reviewers demanded primitive empirical-process upper bounds and matching minimax lower-bound constructions instead of A-frontier-active assumptions.
Archived stat_sa_cate_pointwise/v1 supplies the pointwise nuisance-product Le Cam template and explicitly excludes weak-overlap sequences.
The supplied study-pipeline gaps were mainly LATE, Manski bounds, interval-regression, and proximal-bridge inference questions, so they were treated as augmenting context but not promoted for this ATE overlap-decay topic.
